title: Phase 5: Frontend UI Implementation
description: Implement four new Level 2 UI views in assets/app.js for standards detail, interpretation editor, compliance matrix, and passport management.
cause: Level 2 backend API is complete (7 new actions) but frontend views are missing
Users cannot yet interact with extracted clauses, create interpretations, or view compliance matrices
DPP export API exists but has no UI to trigger it
effect: Delivered a Rushroom-only 'Clauses & DPP' tab (index.html) with four sub-views built in app.js under renderLevel2()
Clauses (l2ClausesView): AI-extract a standard version's clauses and browse them as a table
Interpretations (l2InterpretationsView/l2InterpCard): AI-generate per-clause interpretations, then review/edit status + rationale with a reviewer audit trail
Matrix (l2MatrixView/l2RenderMatrix/l2EditCell): documents x clauses grid, colour-coded by compliance status, click a cell to edit
Passports (l2PassportsView/l2PassportEditor): create DPP records, link interpretations, export JSON-LD (schema.org + ESPR) or JSON
implications: Effort: ~4 hours of frontend development
Risk: Low (all APIs already tested and deployed)
Dependencies: None (backend is ready)
Browser compatibility: Same as existing portal (modern browsers)
Performance: Compliance matrix might need pagination for 100+ clauses
User training: Will need documentation on new workflow
implementation_checklist: Standards detail view - show extracted clauses as sortable table
Interpretation editor - per-clause compliance form with status dropdown
Compliance matrix - grid visualization with color-coded cells
implementation_notes: IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-05. Note: the backend was NOT actually ready as assumed - it was undeployed and did not compile (.on('conflict') is not a Supabase method), had unauthenticated read actions, produced empty interpretations for PDFs, and was missing the entire passport CRUD. All fixed before/alongside the UI; generateInterpretations rewritten to send the document file to Claude (PDF-capable) and batch ~10 clauses per call. Actual function names use the l2* naming under renderLevel2(), not the proposed renderStandardDetails() etc. Verified live end-to-end (RoHS clauses -> interpretations -> matrix -> DPP JSON-LD) and 15/15 UI mock checks.

title: Automated Compliance Matrix Updates
description: Automatically trigger compliance matrix recalculation whenever an interpretation is saved, providing real-time view of compliance changes.
cause: Users need to see compliance impact immediately after saving interpretations
Currently requires manual refresh or page reload to see updated matrix
Could detect patterns (e.g., multiple deviations in same clause across docs)
effect: Add background task to portal-api that triggers on saveInterpretation() completion
Emit WebSocket or server-sent events to connected clients
Update compliance matrix UI in real-time without page reload
Optional: Highlight changed cells in matrix
implications: Effort: ~2-3 hours (backend + frontend)
Risk: Medium (adds real-time complexity, WebSocket dependencies)
Dependencies: WebSocket support or Server-Sent Events
Browser compatibility: Modern browsers only (IE 11 may not support)
Performance: Could impact server if many users editing simultaneously
UX benefit: Highly visible - users see changes immediately
Operational: May need monitoring for WebSocket connection issues
implementation_checklist: Add event emitter to saveInterpretation action
implementation_notes: Depends on PROP-001 (Compliance Matrix UI must exist first)

title: Bulk Interpretation Generation & Approval Workflow
description: Add UI for generating interpretations in bulk (all clauses for a document), with staged approval workflow (draft → review → approved).
cause: Current generateInterpretations() requires manually selecting which clauses to generate for
No staged workflow - once generated, interpretations are immediately saved
Users need to review all proposals before any are committed to database
effect: Add 'Generate for entire document' button in interpretation editor
Create temporary 'draft interpretations' storage (session or temp table)
Show all generated proposals side-by-side before saving any
Allow bulk approve/reject/edit before final save
Add workflow stage tracking: draft → review_pending → approved → saved
implications: Effort: ~5-6 hours (backend staging + UI workflow)
Risk: Medium (adds database schema for draft state)
Dependencies: New 'draft_interpretations' temp table or session storage
Browser compatibility: All modern browsers
Performance: Session storage is fine for <1000 interpretations, otherwise use DB
Data consistency: Temp storage must be cleaned up after session
UX improvement: Significant (users can review all proposals at once)
Operational: Must manage cleanup of abandoned draft sessions
implementation_checklist: Create temp storage for draft interpretations
implementation_notes: Depends on PROP-001. Could use session storage instead of DB for MVP.

title: Deviation Deviation Trend Analysis & Patterns
description: Add analytics view showing deviation patterns: which clauses have most deviations, which documents, trending worse/better over time.
cause: Users have no visibility into compliance trend over time
No way to identify systemic compliance problems (e.g., 'clause 4.11 is always a deviation')
Can't prioritize which compliance issues to fix first (high-impact vs low-impact)
effect: New 'Analytics' tab or section in portal showing:
- Most problematic clauses (sorted by deviation count)
- Most problematic documents (sorted by deviation count)
- Compliance trend line (over time, as versions increase)
- Deviation distribution by severity/type
- New query: SELECT clause_id, COUNT(*) as deviation_count FROM as_operates_interpretations WHERE compliance_status='deviation' GROUP BY clause_id
implications: Effort: ~3-4 hours (new queries + visualization)
Risk: Low (read-only, no mutations)
Dependencies: New database queries (already supported by schema)
Browser compatibility: Modern browsers with chart library (Chart.js, etc.)
Performance: Good (simple GROUP BY queries, indexed on compliance_status)
Data quality: Trends only meaningful after several weeks of data collection
UX value: High (helps prioritize compliance work)
Operational: None (read-only)
implementation_checklist: Add deviation analytics queries to portal-api
Add Chart.js or similar for visualizations
implementation_notes: Can be added independently, doesn't block other features.

title: Automatic DPP Generation from Compliance Matrix
description: Auto-create product passports from compliance data: select product + applicable standards + compliance matrix rows → system generates complete DPP.
cause: Currently product passports are created manually
No automatic population from compliance matrix data
Users must copy-paste compliance data into passport fields
Error-prone process (manual data entry)
effect: New 'Generate from Matrix' button in passport view
UI shows: select product + standards + relevant interpretations
System automatically creates passport_interpretations_links based on selection
Passport pre-populated with compliance data from selected interpretations
User can then add sustainability data and export
implications: Effort: ~2-3 hours (mostly UI + linking logic)
Risk: Low (no new tables, uses existing queries)
Dependencies: PROP-001 (Compliance Matrix), existing passport table
Browser compatibility: All modern browsers
Performance: Fast (simple joins)
User training: Medium (new workflow to explain)
Operational: None
UX improvement: Significant (reduces manual work, improves data accuracy)
implementation_checklist: Add new action: autoGeneratePassport (take product + standards + interpretation IDs)
Add pre-population of compliance data in passport
implementation_notes: Low-risk, high-value feature. Could be Phase 5b.

title: AI-Suggested Compliance Status Based on Document Content
description: When generating interpretations, Claude also suggests compliance_status (compliant/deviation/pending) based on document content analysis.
cause: Currently interpretations are generated without suggested status
Users must manually read through and set status for each
Claude has already read the document - could provide preliminary assessment
effect: Update generateInterpretations action to add compliance_status_suggested field
Claude prompt modified to include: 'Based on the document, what is likely status? (compliant/deviation/pending/na)'
Returned interpretation includes both interpretation_text and suggested status
UI shows suggested status as pre-filled but editable (user can override)
implications: Effort: ~1-2 hours (prompt modification + UI change)
Risk: Low (suggestion only, user decides final status)
Dependencies: PROP-001 (needs UI to show suggestions)
Browser compatibility: None (backend change)
Performance: No impact (Claude already called)
Token usage: Minimal (already generating text, just extracting structured data)
UX improvement: Moderate (saves typing, but user must verify)
Operational: Monitor Claude accuracy over time to calibrate expectations
implementation_checklist: Modify Claude prompt to suggest status
Update JSON schema for generateInterpretations response
Update UI to show suggested_status field
Add explanatory note: 'Claude suggests: X (review and confirm)'
implementation_notes: Easy win, low risk. Good for Phase 5b.

title: Multi-Language Support for Standards & Interpretations
description: Add optional translations for standards and interpretations (English, German, Swedish).
cause: Rushroom is international (Swedish company, EU market)
Standards often referenced in multiple languages
Some users may prefer local language for compliance documents
effect: Add language_code column to standards and interpretations tables
Add translation_group_id to link translations of same content
Claude can auto-generate translations when new interpretation created
UI language selector to switch between translations
DPP export respects selected language
implications: Effort: ~8-10 hours (schema changes + UI + translations)
Risk: Medium (schema change, complexity in query logic)
Dependencies: None
Browser compatibility: All
Performance: Need indexes on language_code and translation_group_id
Data volume: Increases storage (each interpretation now multiple rows)
Operational: Must manage translation quality (Claude + human review)
User training: Medium (new language selector UI)
ROI: High (if users actually need multiple languages)
implementation_checklist: Update DPP export to use selected language
implementation_notes: Large effort. Recommend deferring to Phase 6. Consider MVP: just English for now.

title: Version Control for Interpretations (Diff View)
description: Track changes to interpretations over time (what text changed, who changed it, when). Show diff view comparing versions.
cause: Interpretations can be edited (compliance status, text, rationale, etc.)
No audit trail showing what changed between edits
Can't see if compliance status changed from compliant → deviation
effect: Add interpretation_versions table (like document_versions)
saveInterpretation creates new row instead of UPDATE
Each version has: id, interpretation_id (FK), version_number, created_by, created_at, full_content
UI shows version history + diff view (side-by-side)
Can revert to previous version if needed
implications: Effort: ~4-5 hours (new table + query logic + UI)
Risk: Medium (schema change, versioning logic)
Dependencies: None
Browser compatibility: All (diff library needed: e.g., diff-match-patch)
Storage: Increases DB size (all interpretations kept)
Performance: Query must select specific version, not performance issue
Compliance value: High (audit trail useful for regulatory)
Operational: Data cleanup (old versions can be archived)
implementation_checklist: Modify saveInterpretation to insert new version row
implementation_notes: Good for compliance. Aligns with Level 1 immutability philosophy.

title: Scheduled Compliance Scans & Alerts
description: Run deviation scans on a schedule (e.g., weekly) and email alerts when compliance status changes.
cause: Currently deviation scans are manual (user clicks button)
No proactive monitoring for compliance drift
Users may miss emerging compliance issues
effect: Add scheduled job (via Supabase cron / edge function)
Weekly run of runDeviationScan
Compare new findings to previous scan
Email alert if: new deviations found, or existing deviations resolved
Include summary in email (improved/worsened count)
implications: Effort: ~3-4 hours (scheduling + alerting)
Risk: Low (runs in background)
Dependencies: Email service (already integrated via Supabase Auth)
Performance: Scans run off-peak to minimize load
User experience: Positive (proactive notifications)
Operational: Monitor scheduled tasks for failures
Cost: Minimal (already paying for Supabase)

title: ESPR-Specific Module: Compliance Path Tracing
description: New module showing complete compliance chain for ESPR: standard clause → interpretation → product feature → DPP declaration.
cause: EU regulators will ask: 'How did you ensure compliance with Article 8?'
Need to trace complete path from regulation to implementation to declaration
Current system has pieces but no integrated compliance chain visualization
effect: New 'Compliance Paths' view in portal
Shows: Standard Clause → Implementation in Document → Interpretation → Product Feature → DPP Declaration
User can trace back from DPP claim to its evidence
Generates compliance chain report (PDF) suitable for auditors
implications: Effort: ~6-8 hours (new queries + visualization + PDF generation)
Risk: Medium (regulatory compliance - must be accurate)
Dependencies: All of PROP-001 (needs all pieces in place)
Browser compatibility: Modern browsers + PDF library
Regulatory value: Very high (directly addresses ESPR requirements)
Operational: Must validate accuracy for regulatory submissions
User training: High (complex feature, needs good documentation)
implementation_notes: ESPR deadline 2027 - should start Phase 6 planning. Depends on PROP-001.
